headline: Chess solutions
body_text:
Chess solution 26701 . . . Nf4! 2 exf4 (otherwise Nxd5 or Nxg2 wins) Rh6+ 3 Kg3 Qd3+ 4 Qf3 g4!! when 5 Qxd3 Rh3 is checkmate, while otherwise Black captures White’s queen by 5 . . . gxf3 with a winning material advantage.

Chess solution 26691 f6! wins. The main variation is 1 . . . Qa7+ 2 Kh1 Qxb8 3 e7 Qe8 4 f7! Qxf7 5 e8=Q and wins.

Chess solution 26681 Rc7! (much better than 1 Rc6+? Bd6 when Black can defend) 1...Ne7 (if 1...Be7? 2 R1c6+ Kd5 3 Qxf7 mate) 2 R1c6+ Nxc6 (if 2...Kd5 3 Qxf7 mate) 3 Qxf7+ Kd6 4 Qd7+ Kc5 5 Qxc6+ Kb4 6 Qa4 mate.

Black’s only resource is 1 Rc7 Qxc1+, when White wins easily on material. His a2 pawn will swiftly advance.

Chess solution 26671 Ng6+! hxg6 (if 1 . . . Kg7 2 Nh4+ wins the queen and soon mates) 2 Qh6+ Kg8 3 Rxg6+ Kf7 (if 3 . . . Qxg6 4 Qxg6+ Kh8 5 Qh5+ and 6 Qxd1 wins) 4 Rg7+ Ke8 5 Qe3+! Kd8 6 Qe7+ Kc8 7 Qxc7 mate.

Chess solution 26661 Qh4! Bd7 2 Rxf6! Qxf6 3 d6+! wins the queen. Other defences leave Black a piece down.

Chess solution 26651 Rxd
